query: How does temperature vary with radial distance for electrons, protons, and heavy ions in Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk?

row.paper: Liu, Z.Y., et al., 2024. Juno observations of Jupiter's magnetodisk plasma: Implications for equilibrium and dynamics. JGR Space Physics, 129(11), e2024JA032976. DOI:10.1029/2024JA032976
row.query: How does temperature vary with radial distance for electrons, protons, and heavy ions in Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk?

{'status': 'success', 'results': [{'path': 'data/GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-JUP-COORDS-V1.0', 'pds_version': 'PDS3', 'file_type': 'voldesc.cat', 'dataset_id': 'GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-JUP-COORDS-V1.0', 'dataset_ids': ['GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-JUP-COORDS-V1.0'], 'title': 'Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data', 'fields': {'PDS_VERSION_ID': 'PDS3', 'VOLUME': {'VOLUME_SET_NAME': 'Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data', 'VOLUME_NAME': 'Galileo Orbiter Jupiter SC Traj. Jup.-Centered Coords. Data', 'VOLUME_ID': 'GOPOS_9001', 'PUBLICATION_DATE': '2012-06-19', 'DESCRIPTION': 'CERTIFIED This data set contains the Galileo Orbiter Jupiter SC Traj. Jup.-Centered Coords. data. It tabulates the Galileo spacecraft emphemeris in System III, Jupiter Solar Equatorial, and Jupiter Solar Magnetospheric coordinates. Data are sampled every minute near Jupiter (less frequently at large distances) for all Jupiter orbits.', 'DATA_SET_ID': 'GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-JUP-COORDS-V1.0'}}, 'volume_count': 1}], 'errors': [], 'error': None}

tool_input.summary: Stage 1 (directory walk + label probing) found Galileo PLS derived/moments and fitted-parameter datasets that contain electron/ion temperatures and can be combined with Galileo trajectory data to bin by radial distance and select dawnside local times in Jupiter’s magnetodisk. Also found a Juno JADE moments dataset as an alternative source of electron/ion temperatures at Jupiter (though not as strong for distinct heavy-ion species).

candidates.title: Galileo Orbiter Jupiter PLS Fitted Parameters Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: This PDS4 collection is explicitly a set of fitted parameters for ion species from Galileo PLS (created for Bagenal et al. 2016 plasma sheet survey). It is the most direct source for temperatures of electrons, protons, and multiple ion species (i.e., ‘heavy ions’) as a function of spacecraft location; you can use Galileo trajectory to compute radial distance and local time to isolate the dawnside magnetodisk.

candidates.title: GALILEO JUPITER RTS MOMENT DATA V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Galileo PLS ‘moments’ (PDS3) are a common starting point for radial profiles of plasma parameters at Jupiter (including temperatures for the measured populations, depending on moment type). Useful if you want a mission-archival (often-cited) PDS3 identifier for temperature vs radial distance in the magnetodisk.

candidates.title: Galileo PLS Jupiter RTS Moments Derived Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: This is the PDS4 migration of the Galileo PLS RTS moments (explicitly noted in the collection label modification history). It is the modern equivalent of GO-J-PLS-5-RTS-MOMENTS-V1.0 and supports the same kind of radial temperature profiling once you join with trajectory/local-time selection.

candidates.title: Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: This PDS3 Galileo spacecraft ephemeris in Jupiter-centered coordinate systems (System III, JSE, JSM) is the key companion dataset for computing radial distance (R) and selecting dawnside local times while correlating with PLS electron/proton/heavy-ion temperatures.

candidates.title: Galileo Trajectory Jupiter Centered Coordinates Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: PDS4 trajectory collection for Galileo in common Jovian coordinate systems; use it to derive radial distance and local time (dawnside) to bin temperatures from the PLS fitted-parameter or moments collections.

candidates.title: JUNO JADE
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Juno JADE derived moments provide electron/ion moment quantities at Jupiter; can also be used to build temperature vs radial distance profiles in the magnetodisk for the Juno era. Heavy-ion separation is generally less straightforward than with dedicated multi-species fitted products, so this is a secondary option.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
All ~781 datasets sit directly under data/ with no mission sub-directories.
The mission 'name' from pds_list_missions is the filter keyword to use with list_dataset_dirs.


Dataset naming conventions:
  PDS3 dirs use uppercase mission codes: MESS-, CO-, GO-, JNO-, VG1-, VG2-, MEX-, P10-, P11-, ULY-, NH-, PVO-, etc.
  PDS4 dirs use lowercase names: cassini-, galileo-, juno-, maven-, messenger-, ulysses-, etc.
  Both conventions exist for many missions. Use pds_list_missions to see all available missions and filter keywords.
Slash-encoding in DATA_SET_IDs: directory names cannot contain '/' so PPI replaces it
with '_'. The dir 'JNO-J_SW-JAD-5-CALIBRATED-V1.0' corresponds to DATA_SET_ID
'JNO-J/SW-JAD-5-CALIBRATED-V1.0'. When a DATA_SET_ID contains '/', look for the
directory with '_' in those positions — pds_probe_datasets will return the canonical
DATA_SET_ID with the slashes restored.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (5 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.
  5. pds_resolve_volume(volume_set_path, node='ppi', dataset_id_hint=..., sample=8) — when a parent directory contains many
     numbered sibling volumes (RMS COISS_2xxx, COUVIS_0xxx, ATM
     MROM_*, jnomwr_*, cocirs_*, IMG mrox_*, MSGRMDS_*), this probes
     hint-ranked children in one call and returns per-child
     dataset_ids plus a `best_match` field. Use it instead of
     probing volumes one at a time when you know the target id or
     a discriminating substring of it.

WORKFLOW:
Step 1: Call pds_list_missions(node='ppi') to see all available missions and their filter keywords.
Step 2: Identify which mission(s) are relevant to the query.
Step 3: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<mission_name>') using a `name` field from the mission list as the filter keyword (e.g. filter='cassini'
for PDS4, filter='CO' for PDS3 Cassini, filter='MESS' for MESSENGER). Filter is
mandatory — ~781 entries otherwise.
  STICK TO MISSION KEYWORDS. The `name` and PDS3-prefix fields from pds_list_missions
  are the supported filter values. Do NOT improvise free-text filters like
  'saturn', 'plasmoid', 'magnetotail', 'recon' — those rarely appear in directory
  names and just produce empty listings. Filter by mission, then by instrument prefix
  if needed (CAPS, MAG, MIMI, RPWS, INMS).
Step 4: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch up to 20).
Step 5: If PDS4 bundles are found, call pds_inspect_collections on top 2-3.
Step 6: Return candidates — emit BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 identifiers when a mission has
both (PDS3 dirs like 'CO-S-MAG-...' and PDS4 dirs like 'cassini-mag-cal' coexist
for most major missions on PPI).

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query as 'not a dataset request' — every paper-derived query has at least one
     supporting PDS dataset somewhere in this node's archive, and your job
     is to surface the closest match (with `reasoning` explaining the link).
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
     When a probed voldesc exposes `dataset_ids` with multiple entries,
     match the question's data need to the PRODUCT-TYPE token inside each
     id (typically the third or fourth dash-separated token). Common ones:
         EDR / RAW     = raw observations
         CDR / CALIB   = generic calibrated data
         RDR / TRDR / MRDR / MTRDR / BDR / SCVDR = reduced / derived imagery or spectra
         DDR / LDR     = derived / digital terrain / level-5 products
         SSB           = solar / stellar occultation (Cassini UVIS)
         SPEC / CUBE / WAV = spectra / spectral cubes / waveforms
         QUBE          = VIMS spectral data cube (the VIMS-flavoured "EDR")
     If the query mentions 'occultation' return the SSB id; 'spectra' →
     SPEC; 'calibrated radiance' → CALIB/CDR; 'mosaic / map / derived' →
     DDR/MRDR/MTRDR. When two product types both fit the question (e.g.
     SPEC + SSB for an occultation spectra query), return both candidates.
     Do NOT just return the scalar `dataset_id` and drop the rest — that
     field is only the first id in the voldesc and is often the wrong
     product type for a given query.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When the SAME data is available as both PDS3 and PDS4, return BOTH identifiers — emit one candidate per identifier. Most published papers cite the PDS3 dataset_id (e.g. MESS-E_V_H_SW-MAG-3-CDR-CALIBRATED-V1.0, LRO-L-LROC-5-RDR-V1.0); the PDS4 collection LID is the modern equivalent. Do NOT silently drop the PDS3 form.
  5. Stay under 20 tool calls per query. Soft cap: 15. Hard cap: 20.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
  7. ANTI-THRASHING. Never re-issue a tool call with the same name and the
     same key arguments (path, paths, filter) that you've already issued in
     this trace. The result will be identical — it wastes the budget and
     blocks progress. If you're tempted to re-probe the same set of paths,
     either move to a different tool (inspect_collections, resolve_volume)
     or commit to `final_result` with the best candidate you have so far.
  8. STOP-AT-LEVEL. The output `path` field should point at a dataset or
     bundle directory (containing voldesc.cat or bundle*.xml), NOT at a
     deeper product / DATA / EXTRAS / BROWSE / SHAPEFILE / DTM sub-folder.
     Feature-level retrieval (a specific image of a specific crater) is
     out of scope for this finder; return the dataset and explain in
     `reasoning` how it covers the feature.
  9. After 15 tool calls without converging, ALWAYS prefer emitting your
     current best candidate(s) over running another exploratory call.
     A grounded near-match with a clear reasoning note is more useful
     than a 20-call trace that times out with no `final_result`.

===========================================================
STAGE 2 — DEEPER NODE-SPECIFIC SEARCH (escalation only)
===========================================================

Everything above is STAGE 1: the 5 live-HTTP tools (pds_list_missions / pds_list_dataset_dirs / pds_probe_datasets / pds_inspect_collections / pds_resolve_volume), the per-node workflow, and the Critical Rules ALL belong to Stage 1. Stage 1 reaches bundle/collection level by walking the node's directory tree. Stage 2 below is a SECOND set of tools for refining or rescuing Stage 1 — do NOT call any Stage 2 tool unless one of the escalation triggers below fires.

WHEN TO ESCALATE TO STAGE 2 (any one is enough):
  (a) The query asks for SPECIFIC observations, granules, or product IDs — not just a dataset. Example: 'Cassini ISS images of Mimas between 2009 and 2010' needs per-observation filtering.
  (b) Stage 1 has located the right bundle/collection but you still need to narrow by GEOMETRY, TIME, or a SPATIAL filter (bbox, lat/lon, ring longitude, target body, observation type).
  (c) Stage 1 produced AMBIGUOUS candidates that a faceted query can disambiguate (e.g. multiple instruments with similar names, multiple processing levels).
  (d) Stage 1 cannot reach the node's holdings index (e.g. SBN returns HTTP 403, a directory listing is unavailable). Use the Stage 2 API as a fallback path to candidates.

STAGE 2 TOOLS (this node):
  - pds4search_bundles_tool(keywords=..., investigation=..., instrument=..., target=...): cross-node PDS4 registry bundle search.
  - pds4search_collections_tool(bundle_lid=..., keywords=..., ...): collection search; reuse the Stage 1 bundle LID as a filter when possible.
  - pds4search_products_tool(collection_lid=..., keywords=..., ...): product-level search inside a collection.
  - pds4search_investigations_tool / pds4search_instruments_tool / pds4search_instrument_hosts_tool / pds4search_targets_tool: context-product lookups when user-facing names need to be resolved to LIDs.
  - pds4get_product_tool(lid=...): fetch one product label by LID.
  - pds4crawl_context_product_tool(lid=...): walk a context product's associations to find related bundles/collections.

STAGE 2 RULES:
  1. When you already have a bundle/collection LID from Stage 1, REUSE it as a filter in the Stage 2 call. Stage 2 is refinement, not a fresh search.
  2. Spend the MINIMUM number of additional calls. If a single faceted call answers the question, stop.
  3. If Stage 2 finds nothing or errors out, FALL BACK to the Stage 1 candidates and note in `reasoning` that Stage 2 was attempted and returned no refinement.
  4. The 20-tool-call budget from Stage 1's Critical Rule 5 is the budget for BOTH stages combined. Do NOT reset the counter when entering Stage 2.
  5. Every candidate's `reasoning` field must explicitly name which stage produced it (e.g. 'Stage 1: located bundle X by directory walk' or 'Stage 2: narrowed to product Y via ODE_search_products bbox filter').
How does temperature vary with radial distance for electrons, protons, and heavy ions in Jupiter's dawnside magnetodisk?

missions.name: cassini
missions.description: Cassini at Saturn (CAPS, MAG, MIMI-CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS, RPWS, INMS). Also filter 'CO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: galileo
missions.description: Galileo at Jupiter + flybys of Earth/Venus/asteroids (EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR, HIC, SSD, RSS). Also filter 'GO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: juno
missions.description: Juno at Jupiter (Waves, JADE/JAD, JEDI/JED, FGM, ASC). Also filter 'JNO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: VG1
missions.description: Voyager 1 at Jupiter, Saturn, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg1' for PDS4.

missions.name: VG2
missions.description: Voyager 2 at Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg2' for PDS4.

missions.name: MESS
missions.description: MESSENGER at Mercury (EPPS incl. FIPS & EPS, MAG). Also filter 'messenger' for PDS4 bundle. Target: Mercury.

missions.name: MEX
missions.description: Mars Express (ASPERA-3 incl. ELS/IMA/NPI, MARSIS). Target: Mars.

missions.name: maven
missions.description: MAVEN at Mars (MAG, LPW, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, EUV, ROSE). Target: Mars.

missions.name: P10
missions.description: Pioneer 10 at Jupiter (CPI, CRT, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p10' for PDS4.

missions.name: P11
missions.description: Pioneer 11 at Jupiter and Saturn (CPI, CRT, FGM, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p11' for PDS4.

missions.name: ULY
missions.description: Ulysses at Jupiter and interplanetary (COSPIN, EPAC, HISCALE, SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, URAP, GAS, GRB, SCE, SWICS). Also filter 'ulysses' for PDS4.

missions.name: NH
missions.description: New Horizons at Jupiter and Pluto (PEPSSI, SWAP). Target: Jupiter, Pluto.

missions.name: PVO
missions.description: Pioneer Venus Orbiter (OEFD, OETP, OIMS, OMAG, ONMS, ORPA, ORSE). Also filter 'pvo' for PDS4. Target: Venus.

missions.name: LP
missions.description: Lunar Prospector (MAG, ER — electron reflectometer). Also filter 'lp' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: MGS
missions.description: Mars Global Surveyor (MAG/ER, RSS). Also filter 'mgs' for PDS4. Target: Mars.

missions.name: NEAR
missions.description: NEAR Shoemaker (MAG). Target: Eros, Earth flyby.

missions.name: M10
missions.description: Mariner 10 (MAG, PLS). Target: Mercury.

missions.name: LRO
missions.description: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (CRaTER). Also filter 'lro' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: ODY
missions.description: Mars Odyssey (MARIE — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: MSL
missions.description: Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity (RAD — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: insight
missions.description: InSight (IFG — fluxgate magnetometer). Target: Mars.

missions.name: vex
missions.description: Venus Express (ASPERA-4 ELS, MAG). Target: Venus.

missions.name: ICE
missions.description: International Cometary Explorer (EPAS, MAG, PLAWAV, RADWAV, SWPLAS). Target: Giacobini-Zinner.

missions.name: GIO
missions.description: Giotto (IMS incl. HERS/HIS, JPA, MAG). Target: Halley.

missions.name: VEGA
missions.description: Vega 1 & 2 (MISCHA, PM1, TNM). Target: Halley.

missions.name: DS1
missions.description: Deep Space 1 (PEPE). Target: Borrelly.

missions.name: radiojove
missions.description: Radio JOVE ground-based radio observations of Jupiter.

content.note: Node 'ppi' has no mission sub-directories. Use each mission 'name' as the filter keyword: pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<name>').

results.title: Galileo Plasma Science Instrument Jupiter RTS Moments Derived Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Plasma Science Instrument Jupiter RTS Moments Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Frank, L.A.; Paterson, W.R; Ackerson, K.L.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains data from the Plasma Science instrument               
                (PLS) on the Galileo spacecraft for all Jupiter orbits and Galileo plasma moments.

results.title: Galileo Plasma Science Experiment Jupiter Calibrated Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Plasma Science Experiment Jupiter Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list:
Frank, L.A.; Paterson, W.R.;            
                Ackerson, K.L.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains averaged raw data and spin averaged count rates from selected ion
             and electron channels from the Plasma Science experiment (PLS)
             onboard the Galileo spacecraft for all Jupiter orbits. These data have been averaged
             and reformatted into ASCII tables to facilitate data display and analysis.

results.title: Galileo Orbiter Plasma Science Experiment Jupiter Fitted Parameters Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Orbiter Plasma Science Experiment Jupiter Fitted Parameters Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
This bundle  consists of all of the fitted parameters (both good and bad) for fitting an 
                ion species to the Galileo Plasma Science Experiment (PLS) that were found during a grant 
                from NASA's Jupiter Data Analysis Program (NNX09AE03G). The 'good' results were used to 
                publish:
                
                    Bagenal, F., R. J. Wilson, S. Siler, W. R. Paterson, and W. S. Kurth (2016), 
                    Survey of Galileo plasma observations in Jupiter's plasma sheet,             
                    J. Geophys. Res. Planets, 121, 871-894, doi:10.1002/2016JE005009.            
                
                Please cite this paper and this PDS bundle if you use this data.
            
                Please note: The data in this bundle have been reviewed by the coauthors of Bagenal, et al. 
                2016 and their method and figures passed the journal's peer review. These data have also 
                completed PDS peer review and are certified.

results.title: GALILEO JUPITER RTS MOMENT DATA V1.0

VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: GALILEO JUPITER RTS MOMENT DATA V1.0
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED This data set contains PLS moments from 1996-177T17:00:33.400 to 2002-019T14:44:14.000.

results.title: Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: Galileo Orbiter Jupiter PLS Full-Res. Raw Data
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED This data set contains PLS Full-Res. Raw Data from the Plasma Science instrument(PLS) on the Galileo spacecraft for all Jupiter orbits. These data have been reformatted into ASCII tables to facilitate data processing and analysis.

results.title: Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: Galileo Orbiter Jup HIC Ion Flux Composition Data
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED This data set provides energetic (MeV) ion fluxes for a variety of different Z values (carbon, oxygen, sulfur) derived from the Heavy Ion Counter (HIC)instrument on the Galileo spacecraft. It includes all recorded intervals at Jupiter.

VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: JUNO JADE DERIVED MOMENTS SCIENCE DATA
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED. This volume contains Juno JADE derived moments flight from the Jupiter Orbital Operations phases of the Juno mission. These data have completed PDS peer review and are certified.
CERTIFIED. This volume contains Juno JADE calibrated data, Juno MAG data, and pitch angles from the entire Juno mission. These data have completed PDS peer review and are certified.

collections.title: Galileo PLS Jupiter RTS Moments Derived Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo PLS Jupiter RTS Moments Derived Data Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Frank, L.A.; Paterson, W.R; Ackerson, K.L.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains data from the Plasma Science instrument               
        (PLS) on the Galileo spacecraft for all Jupiter orbits and Galileo plasma moments.
        These data have been reformatted into ASCII tables to facilitate data                   
        processing and analysis.

Modification_Detail.description:
This is the original version of this collection in PDS4. 
          It was migrated to PDS4 from the PDS3 data set GO-J-PLS-5-RTS-MOMENTS-V1.0.

Citation_Information.author_list: Joy, Steven  P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains Galileo spacecraft trajectory data in three           
                coordinate systems commonly used in the analysis of Jovian magnetospheric 
                data. These include System III (1965.0), Jupiter Solar Equatorial (JSE) and         
                Jupiter Solar Magnetospheric (JSM) coordinates from the Jupiter Orbital 
                Operations phases of the Galileo Mission.

results.title: Galileo Orbiter at Jupiter Magnetometer High Resolution Calibrated Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Orbiter at Jupiter Magnetometer High Resolution Calibrated Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list:
Kivelson, Margaret G.; Khurana, Krishan K.; Russell,
                Christopher T.; Walker, Raymond J.; Joy, Steven P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains magnetic field vectors acquired by the           
            Galileo magnetometer (MAG).
                
            This data was previously released as PDS3 data in the GO-J-MAG-3-RDR-HIGHRES-V1.0 (https://doi.org/10.17189/1519667) and 
            GO-J-MAG-3-RDR-MAGSPHERIC-SURVEY-V1.0 (https://doi.org/10.17189/1519668) data sets.

results.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected Data Bundle

Identification_Area.title: Galileo EPD Calibrated Corrected Data Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
These are reprocessed measurements by the Energetic Particle
      Detector (EPD) instrument onboard the Galileo spacecraft that 
      orbited Jupiter and measured intensities of ion and electron 
      radiation in the keV and MeV energy range. This set is 
      version 31 of reprocessing and version 1.0 of PDS delivery.

collections.title: Galileo Orbiter Jupiter PLS Fitted Parameters Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Orbiter Jupiter PLS Fitted Parameters Data Collection

Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains all of the fitted parameters (both good and bad) for 
                fitting an ion species to the Galileo PLS instrument.

collections.title: Galileo Jupiter Trajectory Centered Coordinates Browse Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Jupiter Trajectory Centered Coordinates Browse Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Joy, Steven  P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains browse plots of all 35 Galileo orbits, plus the       
        trajectories of the other spacecraft to visit Jupiter, projected into the     
        JSE X-Y plane (equatorial, X positive towards the Sun).

Modification_Detail.description:
This is the original version of this collection in PDS4. 
          It was migrated to PDS4 from the PDS3 data set GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-JUP-COORDS-V1.0.

collections.title: Galileo Jupiter Trajectory Moon Centered Coordinates Browse Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Jupiter Trajectory Moon Centered Coordinates Browse Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Joy, Steven  P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains detailed Galileo plots
        of all the moon flyby trajectories from orbits.

Modification_Detail.description:
This is the original version of this collection in PDS4. 
          It was migrated to PDS4 from the PDS3 data set GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-MOON-COORDS-V1.0

collections.title: Galileo Jupiter Moon Trajectory Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Jupiter Moon Trajectory Data Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Joy, Steven  P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains data for System III (1965.0) trajectory and Sun and       
        Earth phase angles of Galileo and selected Jovian moons when Galileo was      
        inside 30 Jupiter radii from Jupiter. Trajectories are sampled every 20       
        seconds.

Modification_Detail.description:
This is the original version of this collection in PDS4. 
          It was migrated to PDS4 from the PDS3 data set GO-J-POS-6-MOON-TRAJ-JUP-CRDS-V1.0.

collections.title: Galileo Jupiter Trajectory Rotor Attitude Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Jupiter Trajectory Rotor Attitude Data Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Joy, Steven  P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains the attitude data for the rotor of the Galileo       
        spacecraft. The data provided are derived from the Attitude and               
        Articulation Control System (AACS) data downlink as provided by the           
        Galileo project to the magnetometer team. This collection covers              
        portions of the Jupiter Approach (JA) and all orbit operation mission         
        phases (J0-J35).

Modification_Detail.description:
This is the original version of this collection in PDS4. 
          It was migrated to PDS4 from the PDS3 data set GO-J-POS-6-REDR-ROTOR-ATTITUDE-V1.0.

collections.title: Galileo Trajectory Jupiter Centered Coordinates Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Trajectory Jupiter Centered Coordinates Data Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Joy, Steven  P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Galileo trajectory data in various
        Jupiter centered coordinate systems (System III 1965.0, Jupiter Solar
        Equatorial, Jupiter Solar Magnetospheric) for all orbit operation mission         
        phases.

Modification_Detail.description:
This is the original version of this collection in PDS4. 
          It was migrated to PDS4 from the PDS3 data set GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-JUP-COORDS-V1.0.

collections.title: Galileo Jupiter Trajectory Moon Centered Coordinates Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Galileo Jupiter Trajectory Moon Centered Coordinates Data Collection

Citation_Information.author_list: Joy, Steven  P.; Mafi, Joseph N.
Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains Galileo trajectory data in moon (Amalthea, Io,       
        Europa, Ganymede, Callisto) centered coordinates for all of the near          
        satellite encounters. Ephemeris data are provided every two seconds for       
        approximately one hour about closest approach.

Modification_Detail.description:
This is the original version of this collection in PDS4. 
          It was migrated to PDS4 from the PDS3 data set GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-MOON-COORDS-V1.0.

results.title: Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data

VOLUME.VOLUME_SET_NAME: Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data
VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: Galileo Orbiter Jupiter SC Traj. Jup.-Centered Coords. Data
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED This data set contains the Galileo Orbiter Jupiter SC Traj. Jup.-Centered Coords. data. It tabulates the Galileo spacecraft emphemeris in System III, Jupiter Solar Equatorial, and Jupiter Solar Magnetospheric coordinates. Data are sampled every minute near Jupiter (less frequently at large distances) for all Jupiter orbits.

parts.args: {"summary":"Stage 1 (directory walk + label probing) found Galileo PLS derived/moments and fitted-parameter datasets that contain electron/ion temperatures and can be combined with Galileo trajectory data to bin by radial distance and select dawnside local times in Jupiter’s magnetodisk. Also found a Juno JADE moments dataset as an alternative source of electron/ion temperatures at Jupiter (though not as strong for distinct heavy-ion species).","candidates":[{"path":"data/go-pls-jup-fitted-parameters/","dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:go-pls-jup-fitted-parameters:data","title":"Galileo Orbiter Jupiter PLS Fitted Parameters Data Collection","pds_version":"PDS4","mission":"galileo","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: This PDS4 collection is explicitly a set of fitted parameters for ion species from Galileo PLS (created for Bagenal et al. 2016 plasma sheet survey). It is the most direct source for temperatures of electrons, protons, and multiple ion species (i.e., ‘heavy ions’) as a function of spacecraft location; you can use Galileo trajectory to compute radial distance and local time to isolate the dawnside magnetodisk."},{"path":"data/GO-J-PLS-5-RTS-MOMENTS-V1.0/","dataset_id":"GO-J-PLS-5-RTS-MOMENTS-V1.0","title":"GALILEO JUPITER RTS MOMENT DATA V1.0","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"galileo","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: Galileo PLS ‘moments’ (PDS3) are a common starting point for radial profiles of plasma parameters at Jupiter (including temperatures for the measured populations, depending on moment type). Useful if you want a mission-archival (often-cited) PDS3 identifier for temperature vs radial distance in the magnetodisk."},{"path":"data/galileo-pls-jup-derived/","dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:galileo-pls-jup-derived:data","title":"Galileo PLS Jupiter RTS Moments Derived Data Collection","pds_version":"PDS4","mission":"galileo","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: This is the PDS4 migration of the Galileo PLS RTS moments (explicitly noted in the collection label modification history). It is the modern equivalent of GO-J-PLS-5-RTS-MOMENTS-V1.0 and supports the same kind of radial temperature profiling once you join with trajectory/local-time selection."},{"path":"data/GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-JUP-COORDS-V1.0/","dataset_id":"GO-J-POS-6-SC-TRAJ-JUP-COORDS-V1.0","title":"Galileo at Jupiter Fields and Particles Science Data","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"galileo","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: This PDS3 Galileo spacecraft ephemeris in Jupiter-centered coordinate systems (System III, JSE, JSM) is the key companion dataset for computing radial distance (R) and selecting dawnside local times while correlating with PLS electron/proton/heavy-ion temperatures."},{"path":"data/galileo-traj-jup/","dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:galileo-traj-jup:data-sc-traj-jup-coords","title":"Galileo Trajectory Jupiter Centered Coordinates Data Collection","pds_version":"PDS4","mission":"galileo","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: PDS4 trajectory collection for Galileo in common Jovian coordinate systems; use it to derive radial distance and local time (dawnside) to bin temperatures from the PLS fitted-parameter or moments collections."},{"path":"data/JNO-J-JAD-5-MOMENTS-V1.0/","dataset_id":"JNO-J-JAD-5-MOMENTS-V1.0","title":"JUNO JADE","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"juno","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: Juno JADE derived moments provide electron/ion moment quantities at Jupiter; can also be used to build temperature vs radial distance profiles in the magnetodisk for the Juno era. Heavy-ion separation is generally less straightforward than with dedicated multi-species fitted products, so this is a secondary option."}]}
